questions.prompt: Where should the Squidex GitHub repo live?
questions.description: Currently the origin remote points to Mohit-Patil/clawdex-mobile. You'll need your own repo to publish from. Should I fork it to your GitHub account (drewsepeczi/squidex-mobile)?

questions.prompt: How should the npm package be scoped/published?
questions.description: The root package name is 'squidex-mobile' with public access. It's currently unpublished. Under what npm username or scope should it be published?
questions.recommendedAnswer: Publish as 'squidex-mobile' under your npm account

questions.prompt: How are you distributing the mobile app?
questions.description: The mobile app currently ships via TestFlight (iOS) and direct APK (Android). There's also an EAS build setup. Do you want to keep these channels, change them, or start fresh?
questions.recommendedAnswer: Keep TestFlight + APK for now; update branding before next submission

questions.prompt: What's the status of the website/domains?
questions.description: The code references getclawdex.com and getsquidex.com. The site/ directory has static HTML. Which domains are yours and what should we point to?
questions.recommendedAnswer: Migrate site to getsquidex.com, deprecate getclawdex.com

questions.prompt: What's the priority order for the rebrand work?
questions.description: There are ~55 files with stale 'clawdex' references plus external infra (repo rename, npm publish, domains). What order makes sense?
questions.recommendedAnswer: 1) Wire protocol strings (risk of breakage), 2) GitHub repo + npm, 3) File/asset rename, 4) Docs + site, 5) CI/CD cleanup

questions.prompt: Anything you want to defer or skip?
questions.description: Not everything needs to change. Storage keys like 'clawdex-app-settings.json' could stay as-is if you don't want to break existing user state. Docs can be cleaned up gradually. What's OK to leave for later?
questions.recommendedAnswer: Change wire protocol and visible branding now; defer internal storage keys if backward compat matters
